title: Reverse a String
description: Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^5. s[i] is a printable ascii character.
output_format: An array of characters reversed in-place.
optimal_solution: Two-pointer approach: Swap the first and last elements, then move toward the middle.

title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
input_format: An array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def twoSum(self, nums: List[int], target: int) -> List[int]:
optimal_solution: Use a hash map to store the complement of each number and its index.

title: Fibonacci Number
description: The Fibonacci numbers, commonly denoted F(n) form a sequence, called the Fibonacci sequence, such that each number is the sum of the two preceding ones, starting from 0 and 1.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def fib(self, n: int) -> int:
optimal_solution: Use recursion with memoization or iterative approach for O(n).

title: Move Zeroes
description: Given an integer array nums, move all 0's to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.
optimal_solution: Use two pointers: one to track the last non-zero element.

title: Palindrome Check
description: A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 2 * 10^5. s consists only of printable ASCII characters.

test_cases.input: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def isPalindrome(self, s: str) -> bool:
optimal_solution: Clean the string and use two pointers to compare characters from both ends.

title: Sum of Array (Recursive)
description: Given an array of integers, find the sum of all elements using recursion.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def arraySum(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
optimal_solution: sum(nums) = nums[0] + sum(nums[1:])

title: Check Empty Object
description: Given an object or an array, return if it is empty. An empty object contains no key-value pairs. An empty array contains no elements.
constraints: obj is a valid JSON object or array.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def isEmpty(self, obj: any) -> bool:
optimal_solution: Use Object.keys(obj).length === 0 for objects and obj.length === 0 for arrays.
time_complexity: O(N) for objects (to get keys), O(1) for arrays

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def maxProfit(self, prices: List[int]) -> int:
optimal_solution: Keep track of the minimum price seen so far and the maximum profit found.

title: Group Anagrams
description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: 1 <= strs.length <= 10^4. 0 <= strs[i].length <= 100. strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.
output_format: An array of arrays of strings.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def groupAnagrams(self, strs: List[str]) -> List[List[str]]:
optimal_solution: Use a hash map with sorted strings as keys or a character frequency count array.
time_complexity: O(N * K log K) where K is the max length of a string

title: 3Sum
description: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0.
output_format: An array of arrays of triplets.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def threeSum(self, nums: List[int]) -> List[List[int]]:
optimal_solution: Sort the array and use a three-pointer approach (one fixed, two moving from ends).
space_complexity: O(log N) to O(N) depending on sorting implementation

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals, and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def merge(self, intervals: List[List[int]]) -> List[List[int]]:
optimal_solution: Sort intervals by start time and merge greedily.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: 0 <= s.length <= 5 * 10^4. s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
optimal_solution: Use a sliding window with a hash map to store the index of each character.
space_complexity: O(min(N, M)) where M is the size of the character set

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given the array nums after a rotation and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums, or -1 if it is not in nums.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 5000. -10^4 <= nums[i] <= 10^4. All values are unique.
input_format: An array of integers `nums` and a `target` int.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def search(self, nums: List[int], target: int) -> int:
optimal_solution: Modified binary search: determine which half is sorted and check if target lies there.

title: Coin Change
description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount representing a total amount of money. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount.
input_format: An array of coins and an amount int.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def coinChange(self, coins: List[int], amount: int) -> int:
optimal_solution: Dynamic Programming bottom-up approach: dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i-coin] + 1).

title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal (Recursive)
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the preorder traversal of its nodes' values using recursion.
constraints: Number of nodes is in range [0, 100]. -100 <= Node.val <= 100.
input_format: The root of a binary tree.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def preorderTraversal(self, root: Optional[TreeNode]) -> List[int]:
optimal_solution: Recursive call: process node, then left child, then right child.
space_complexity: O(N) (worst case for skewed tree recursion stack)

title: Container With Most Water
description: Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container, such that the container contains the most water.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def maxArea(self, height: List[int]) -> int:
optimal_solution: Two-pointer approach: move the pointer with shorter height inward.

title: Regular Expression Matching
description: Given an input string s and a pattern p, implement regular expression matching with support for '.' and '*'.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 20. 1 <= p.length <= 20. s and p contain only lowercase letters and dots/asterisks.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def isMatch(self, s: str, p: str) -> bool:
optimal_solution: Dynamic Programming with dp[i][j] representing if s[0...i] matches p[0...j].

title: N-Queens
description: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> List[List[str]]:
optimal_solution: Backtracking: place queens row by row, checking column and diagonal constraints.

title: Longest Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters '(' and ')', find the length of the longest valid (well-formed) parentheses substring.
starter_code_py:
class Solution:
    def longestValidParentheses(self, s: str) -> int:
optimal_solution: Use a stack to keep track of indices or dynamic programming.

title: Custom Promise Implementation
description: Implement a class MyPromise that mimics the behavior of built-in Promise including then, catch, and resolve/reject states.
input_format: Function executor with resolve and reject.
optimal_solution: Use state machine (Pending, Fulfilled, Rejected) and a task queue (Macrotask/Microtask) simulation.
space_complexity: O(H) where H is the number of then handlers

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Reverse a singly linked list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000]. -1000 <= Node.val <= 1000.
input_format: A linked list represented as an array of node values.
optimal_solution: Iteratively reverse pointers using prev/current variables. For array representation reverse the array in-place.

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Given head, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4].
input_format: A linked list (array form) with optional cycle indicator; for testing, input is array and an index where tail connects (-1 if none).
optimal_solution: Use Floyd's Tortoise and Hare (fast/slow pointers) to detect a cycle in O(N) time and O(1) space.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.
constraints: m == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 300.
input_format: 2D grid as nested arrays of '1' and '0'.
optimal_solution: DFS/BFS flood fill marking visited cells; count root calls.

title: Course Schedule (Topological Sort)
description: There are numCourses labeled from 0 to numCourses-1 and prerequisites pairs; determine if you can finish all courses.
input_format: An integer numCourses and an array of prerequisite pairs.
output_format: Boolean indicating if a topological ordering exists.
optimal_solution: Use Kahn's algorithm (indegree + queue) or DFS cycle detection to determine if DAG exists.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array nums and a sliding window size k, return the maximum of each sliding window.
input_format: An array nums and integer k.
output_format: Array of maximums for each window.
optimal_solution: Maintain a decreasing deque of indices; pop indices outside window and smaller elements.

title: Binary Search (Iterative)
description: Implement standard binary search to find target in a sorted array.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 10^5. nums sorted in ascending order.
input_format: A sorted array nums and integer target.
output_format: Index of target or -1 if not found.
optimal_solution: Standard iterative binary search using left/right pointers and mid calculation.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
input_format: An array nums and integer k.
optimal_solution: Use Quickselect for average O(N) or maintain a min-heap of size k for O(N log k).

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted array of integers, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence.
optimal_solution: Use a hash set: for each number that is the start of sequence (num-1 not in set), expand forward counting length.

title: Jump Game
description: Given an array of non-negative integers, you are initially positioned at the first index of the array. Each element represents your maximum jump length at that position. Determine if you are able to reach the last index.
output_format: Boolean indicating if last index is reachable.
optimal_solution: Greedy: iterate and keep the farthest reachable index; if index exceeds farthest return false.
